practices.name: workshop
practices.description: Commissions, builds, materials, joinery, finishing, deliveries.

practices.name: money
practices.description: Cashflow, invoicing, chasing balances, tool/timber spend, rent.

obs.body: Walnut sideboard for the Hewitts confirmed. Quoted them six weeks start to finish, should be tidy. Deposit cleared this morning.
Hewitt deposit landed, 900 in. Drove out to the yard for the carcass oak, came back with two extra boards of figured walnut as well. Pretty stuff.
Dog got me up at half five again. Walked her round the rec before the workshop. Frost still on the cars.
Cut the sideboard panels to rough length. Good grain match on the front. Radio doing the wartime requests hour, half listened.
Mara asked if I'd do her a bookshelf for the alcove. Said yes, mates' rates obviously, she's known me twenty years. Sketched it on the back of an invoice.
Invoiced the Pearsons for the bench balance, 540. That job closed out three weeks ago, about time.
Dust extractor packed in halfway through planing. Just stopped. Whacked it, nothing. Bag's not full. Annoying.
Extractor working again this morning, no idea what I did. Reseated the hose maybe. Back planing the walnut.
Lunch at Greco's again, the usual. Read the paper. Quiet Saturday.
Hewitt sideboard. Said six weeks, end of week one and the drawer stock has cupped on me, has to be re-dimensioned. Behind already but it'll catch up.
Rent due Friday, 650. Statement says I'm fine for it but only just. The Pearson balance hasn't landed yet.
Started Mara's bookshelf carcass on the side between sideboard glue-ups. Forgot how much oak even a small unit eats. Three boards gone and that's just the sides.
Pearson balance finally in, 540. Rent covered. Breathe.
Bank balance grim until that Pearson money cleared. Two outstanding invoices I need to chase. Rent took the cushion.
Sideboard glue-up today. Squeeze-out everywhere and a panel slipped on me, dried proud. Have to plane it flat and hope.
Van booked in for MOT next Tuesday. Probably needs a tyre too, the front nearside's worn.
Planed that proud panel back. Lost a hair of thickness and there's a faint glue line now if you catch the light. Hewitts won't notice. I notice.
Dave rang, his mate wants a coffee table, walnut, live edge. Told Dave I'd do him a good price. Roughed a number on the back of an envelope.
Mara's bookshelf was meant to be a weekend filler. It's eaten three evenings and I'm shaping the face frame still. Should've kept it simpler.
Craft fair at the old market hall in four weeks. Booked a table, 45 quid. Might shift some of the small stuff, boards and the little stools.
Humidity's been mad this week, everything's moving. Blaming the damp for the sideboard door not sitting flush. Workshop felt like a greenhouse.
Quoted a wardrobe job for a couple in the new builds. Sent it over at 2,200. Haven't heard back, they winced a bit in person.
Sideboard. Today was meant to be the day it shipped. It is not shipping today. Hinges arrived wrong size, reordered.
Greco's with the dog tied outside. Soup of the day was leek. Fine.
Mara's bookshelf done and delivered, she loves it. Used more oak than I'd reckoned on for the alcove sizing.
Two invoices still unpaid, the Lowes one is now five weeks. Sent a polite nudge. Hate doing it.
Right hinges came. Hung the sideboard doors finally. Looks the part. Said six weeks, we're at three and a bit weeks just for this and it's not finished, still needs finishing oil.
Started the live-edge coffee table for Dave's mate. The slab had a shake running deeper than I thought. More filling than building.
Wardrobe couple came back, want to go ahead but knocked me to 2,000. Deposit of 600 to follow. I'll take it, work's work.
Wardrobe deposit in, 600. Straight to the yard for the sheet ply and carcass timber, and grabbed a lovely burr elm slab while I was there.
Sanded and oiled the Hewitt sideboard. First coat looks rich. Delivery booked for Thursday. Six week job, we're at week four, close enough to call it a win in my head.
Old schoolfriend Col helping me in the shop the next couple of days, owed him a favour years back and he likes pottering. Extra pair of hands on the wardrobe carcass.
Delivered the Hewitt sideboard. They're thrilled. Balance invoiced, 900. One off the list.
Hewitt balance paid on the spot, 900. Good clients. Account looks healthy for once.
Col and I got the wardrobe carcass dry-fitted. Went quick with two of us. He left at three, back to his own life.
Dave's coffee table. The favour table has now eaten most of a week and I've not touched the wardrobe doors. The shake in the slab keeps revealing more of itself.
Van passed the MOT, just needed the one tyre. 90 quid all in. Better than feared.
Lowe invoice finally paid after the second nudge. Five and a half weeks. Logged it.
A delivery of pre-cut drawer sides arrived warped, properly bowed. Sending the lot back, won't use them. Supplier apologised, replacement coming.
Wardrobe doors marked out. Couple wants soft-close runners, ordered those, 60 quid out the deposit.
Looked at Dave's coffee table over a cuppa. The shake in the slab keeps eating hours, deeper every cut.
Greco's. The owner's getting a new awning. Riveting stuff. Ham and cheese.
Money's gone tight again. Wardrobe deposit's mostly spent on stock and runners, nothing new invoiced this week, rent looms again next Friday.
Stared at the bank app over coffee. Tight. Need to invoice something soon. Chasing the wardrobe couple's mind off the price.
Rushing the coffee table glue-up to free the bench and I cross-threaded a clamp and the top racked. Pulled it apart wet, re-did it, lost the morning. Should've waited.
Replacement drawer sides came, straight this time. Back on the wardrobe drawers.
Dog rolled in fox muck on the green. Bath in the yard with the cold hose. She was thrilled, I was not.
Coffee table finally out to Dave's mate. He paid the cheap number happily. That shake cost me days of filling.
Wardrobe was due done this Friday per my own plan. It will not be. Doors need a second go at the bevel and I've the fair to prep for.
Rent paid, 650. Cleared but it took the wardrobe couple's deposit remainder to do it. Living invoice to invoice this stretch.
Made a batch of small oak boards and three stools for the craft fair. Quick work, satisfying for once, no client breathing on it.
Blamed the weather again for a board cupping in the rack, then remembered I'd leant it against the radiator myself. Not the weather this time. My fault.
Money's still pinched. Nothing's cleared since the Hewitt balance and that feels like a month ago. Wardrobe balance is the next real payday, whenever it ships.

In a hurry to get wardrobe doors hung before I lose the light and the week, I drilled the hinge cups 2mm proud and blew through the veneer. One door scrapped, re-cutting a new blank tomorrow. Costly half hour.
Re-cut the wardrobe door blank from the spare ply. Glad I had it. Slower but right.
Craft fair day at the market hall. Decent footfall. Sold all three stools and most of the boards, 280 cash in the tin. Good day out, chatted to a turner with a nice stall.
Fair takings 280, all cash. Small but it's the first money in for two weeks and it'll get me to the wardrobe balance. Banked it.
Wardrobe doors hung and adjusted, runners in, soft-close working. Was meant to be a five week job, we're past seven. Reads done though.
Delivered and fitted the wardrobe at the new builds. Couple delighted, no quibble about it running on. Balance invoiced, 1,400.
Wardrobe balance paid same evening, 1,400. Account finally back in the black with room. First time it's felt easy in weeks.
New enquiry, a dining table for eight, oak, big job. Roughing a quote. Telling myself eight weeks this time and meaning it.
Wardrobe paid, so I detoured to the yard and bought a slab of elm I've no job for. Good stuff. It was there.
Greco's, the usual, dog under the table. New awning's up. Looks smart. Slow morning, earned it.
